newsSinceJun6: Shares fell another -6.16% on June 9 as the Nasdaq noncompliance overhang (late March-31 Form 10-Q, formal delisting-threat notice covered June 4) presses into an imminent fiscal Q2 report (June 9-16 window; consensus EPS -$0.04 on ~$2.0M revenue). Backdrop: prior quarter revenue fell 9% YoY to $1.15M with GAAP net loss widening to $8.4M, pending shareholder lawsuits, and a May 29 $21.2M Las Vegas warehouse purchase pivoting capital toward AI-infrastructure/data-center buildout rather than robot commercialization. Today's slide is fundamental/governance de-risking, not flow.

sources.label: Yahoo Finance - Why Richtech Robotics (RR) Is Down 6.0% After Nasdaq Noncompliance Notice Over Delayed 10-Q Filing
Foreign Policy Journal (2026-06-04) - Richtech Robotics Faces Delisting Threat As Nasdaq Noncompliance Notice Sends Shares Sliding
StockTitan - Richtech Robotics completes $21.2M Las Vegas warehouse acquisition (May 29, 2026)
Benzinga - Richtech Robotics earnings estimates (Q2 FY26, EPS -$0.04)

verdicts.rankRationale: On a robotics builder-purity list, a live Nasdaq delisting threat from a late 10-Q plus declining revenue (-9% YoY to $1.15M) directly undercuts the commercialization-evidence leg; today's -6% slide is governance-driven de-risking, so the rerating-discipline 'sell-off improves asymmetry' exemption does not apply because the thesis is not intact.
verdicts.bull: If the delayed 10-Q is filed and fiscal Q2 (due June 9-16) shows ADAM/Scorpion deployment traction, the NVIDIA Isaac/Jetson Thor positioning at a $0.71B cap and -34% YTD offers genuine torque; the $21.2M Las Vegas facility adds GPU/data-collection optionality for World Action Model training.
verdicts.neutral: Sub-$2M quarterly revenue means the humanoid/service-robot story is still pre-commercial; until filings are current and the lawsuit overhang clears, the stock trades on compliance headlines and retail flow rather than design wins.
verdicts.bear: Late filings, a formal Nasdaq noncompliance notice, widening losses ($8.4M last quarter on $1.15M revenue), shareholder suits, and capital diverted to a warehouse/data-center pivot point to escalating dilution and possible delisting before the robotics thesis can ever be tested.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Fiscal Q2 earnings and delinquent 10-Q filing in the June 9-16 window, restoring Nasdaq compliance.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Failure to cure the late filing leading to Nasdaq delisting, compounded by cash burn and dilution at a sub-$2M revenue run-rate.
